subject: Fwd: LSJ Island
body:
FYI

Sent from my iPhone

Begin forwarded message:

> From: Cecile de Jongh <ceciledejongh@yahoo.com>
> Date: May 17, 2008 5:54:10 AM EDT
> To: <dkiesq@aol.com>
> Subject: FW: LSJ Island
>

>
>
> -----Original Message-----
> From: "Robert Mathes" <robertmathes@mac.com>
> To: "Cecile de Jongh" <ceciledejongh@yahoo.com>
> Sent: 5/16/08 6:24 PM
> Subject: Re: LSJ Island
>
> FL:
> I hope that's the case. The picture has the sand displayed on the
> east beach in nice neat piles that look for all the world like
> deposits for beach nourishment. Typically when you stockpile sand for
> a cement plant you'll see a large mound near the plant operation not
> decorously mounded piles on the beach. I did speak with Algem this
> morning and her story coincides with yours. Just remind your boss
> that the construction activity on his island engenders more
> unsolicited private citizen observation and comment than any project
> in the VI.
>
> RM
>
>
> On May 16, 2008, at 2:43 PM, Cecile de Jongh wrote:
>
>> Bob,
>>
>>
>> With regard to LSJ, I spoke to Darren and he said that the sand
>> that you have a picture of was brought in for the cement batching
>> area that they have.  Heavy Materials ran out of sand so they
>> ordered sand for their cement mix.  The sand was NOT from the beach
>> and had nothing to do with the beach.
>>
>> C
>

body_html:
FYI

Sent from my iPhone

Begin forwarded message:

From: Cecile de Jongh <ceciledejongh@yahoo.com>
Date: May 17, 2008 5:54:10 AM EDT
To: <dkiesq@aol.com>
Subject: FW: LSJ Island



-----Original Message-----
From: "Robert Mathes" <robertmathes@mac.com>
To: "Cecile de Jongh" <ceciledejongh@yahoo.com>
Sent: 5/16/08 6:24 PM
Subject: Re: LSJ Island

FL:
I hope that's the case. The picture has the sand displayed on the  
east beach in nice neat piles that look for all the world like  
deposits for beach nourishment. Typically when you stockpile sand for  
a cement plant you'll see a large mound near the plant operation not  
decorously mounded piles on the beach. I did speak with Algem this  
morning and her story coincides with yours. Just remind your boss  
that the construction activity on his island engenders more  
unsolicited private citizen observation and comment than any project  
in the VI.

RM


On May 16, 2008, at 2:43 PM, Cecile de Jongh wrote:

Bob,


With regard to LSJ, I spoke to Darren and he said that the sand  
that you have a picture of was brought in for the cement batching  
area that they have.  Heavy Materials ran out of sand so they  
ordered sand for their cement mix.  The sand was NOT from the beach  
and had nothing to do with the beach.

C

Robert S. Mathes
Commissioner
Government of the US Virgin Islands
Department of Planning & Natural Resources
Cyril E. King Airport
